basics.name: Xinye Zhao
basics.summary: Ph.D. student in Computer Science and Engineering at the University of Notre Dame, working on software engineering security, LLM systems, and AI-assisted vulnerability detection.

work.name: University of Notre Dame
work.position: Graduate Research Assistant, Computer Science and Engineering
work.summary: Research on efficient LLM inference and AI-assisted vulnerability detection.
highlights: Designed a KVCache sharing framework for semantically similar prompts to minimize recomputation.
Developing a multi-agent system that simulates human hacker behavior to detect vulnerabilities in C/C++.

work.name: University of Notre Dame
work.position: Graduate Research Assistant, Computer Science and Engineering
highlights: Designed a KVCache sharing framework for semantically similar prompts to minimize recomputation.
Integrated RoPE into vector embeddings to preserve positional information during fuzzy token matching.
Achieved a 6.25x speedup and a 42% reduction in memory usage during LLM inference.

work.name: Georgia Institute of Technology
work.position: Graduate Research Assistant, Electrical and Computer Engineering
work.summary: Research on IoT smart-speaker traffic and eavesdropping behavior.
highlights: Studied smart-speaker network behavior with a focus on eavesdropping and targeted advertising.
Built an automatic testing system with tablets and smart speakers to analyze behavior.
Leveraged FFT-based temporal features to analyze traffic from pcap files.

work.name: Georgia Institute of Technology
work.summary: Research on machine learning for single-cell sequencing data.
highlights: Contributed to scDisInFact for batch effect removal, key gene detection, and perturbation prediction.
Used multiple encoders to disentangle shared and unshared biological factors.

work.name: Georgia Institute of Technology
work.summary: Research on doublet detection in scRNA-seq data.
highlights: Developed scMODD for doublet detection in scRNA-seq data.
Modeled gene expression with Negative Binomial and Zero-Inflated Negative Binomial distributions.

work.name: Bytedance
highlights: Developed the Popover Toolbar for Lark Documentation's Table component.
Implemented end-to-end tests using Mocha, Puppeteer, and Chai.
Designed UI styles and buttons with Styled-Components.

publications.name: scMODD: A model-driven algorithm for doublet identification in single-cell RNA-sequencing data
publications.summary: Model-driven framework for doublet identification in single-cell RNA-sequencing data.

publications.name: scDisInFact: Disentangled Learning for the Integration and Prediction of Multi-Batch, Multi-Condition Single-Cell RNA-Sequencing Data
publications.summary: Disentangled learning framework for integrating and predicting multi-batch, multi-condition single-cell RNA-sequencing data.

publications.name: SemShareKV: Efficient KVCache Sharing for Semantically Similar Prompts via Token-Level LSH Matching
publications.summary: Semantic-aware KV cache sharing framework for efficient LLM inference with token-level LSH matching.

publications.name: Security in the Age of AI Teammates: An Empirical Study of Agentic Pull Requests on GitHub
publications.summary: Empirical study of security-related agentic pull requests on GitHub.

portfolio.name: Raman Spectra Signal Automatic Detection
portfolio.description: Developed a method to denoise and detect Raman spectra signals using a modified SSD model with a double-headed network and non-local block.

portfolio.name: C++ Based 3D Pacman Game
portfolio.description: Built a 3D Pacman game with OpenCV, DFS-based maze logic, ghost tracking, multithreading, and lighting effects.
